message:
fix(postgres): drain to ReadyForQuery on ErrorResponse to prevent connection desynchronization

When the Postgres wire protocol returns an ErrorResponse (byte 'E') during
extended-query execution, the server continues sending messages until a
ReadyForQuery (byte 'Z') frame arrives. Previously, we returned the error
immediately without consuming the trailing messages, leaving the TCP stream
mid-protocol. Subsequent queries on the same connection would then read stale
frames from the prior failed command, causing cascading parse failures or
silent data corruption.

Now both execute_simple and execute_extended drain the message stream up to
and including the ReadyForQuery frame before returning the error, and update
the connection's transaction_status byte so callers see the correct
in-transaction / idle / failed state.

- Affected paths: simple-query and extended-query execute loops
- Both paths use identical drain logic for consistency
- On drain read failure, breaks immediately (connection is already broken)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
